_comment: Golden evaluation set for the S&P 500 RAG vault. expected_tickers = the tickers whose notes SHOULD be retrieved to answer well (relationship ground truth from filings + manual overrides). Recall@k measures how many of these the retriever surfaces. Curate/expand this as an analyst task.

questions.question: Which companies supply memory chips to NVIDIA?
What components does Apple buy from Qualcomm, Skyworks, and Qorvo?
Which cloud and hyperscaler companies buy NVIDIA GPUs?
What semiconductors does Tesla use in its vehicles?
Which chipmakers supply Dell's servers and PCs?
Who supplies Verizon's 5G and networking equipment?
What does Arista Networks source from Broadcom?
Which automakers depend on NXP Semiconductors?
Which companies supply Microsoft's data centers?
What chips does Super Micro use to build AI servers?
Who buys networking silicon from Broadcom?
What does Ford buy from Qualcomm and ON Semiconductor?
Which company is the most systemically central in the chip supply chain?
Which memory and storage makers have the most bullish sentiment?

_graph_comment: Deterministic regression guards for the graph-metric router (graph_qa): ranking, aggregate, and multi-hop set algebra. Unlike the recall@k questions above, these assert the EXACT selected member set (relationship/scope derived — stable across data refreshes) and, for aggregates, that the computed value recomputes consistently against an independent reducer (drift-proof vs live P/E / market cap). expected_set = the set graph_qa._select_set must return. Update these when the universe or extracted relationships change.

graph_queries.question: What is the average P/E of NVIDIA's competitors?
What is the average P/E of the competitors of NVIDIA's suppliers?
What is the total market cap of the hyperscalers?
What is the median sentiment of semiconductor companies?
How many competitors does Apple have?
Which memory and storage makers have the most bullish sentiment?
Which of NVIDIA's suppliers has the lowest P/E?
Which of NVIDIA's competitors have a P/E under 30?
Which semiconductor companies have a market cap over $200B?
Which of Apple's competitors have sentiment above 0.5?
